planets.name: Mbateman
planets.summary: Archive cluster around mbateman: 5145 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Mbateman receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt: when babies are trusted to practice doing hard things from early on - be it sleeping without bars around them, eating solid pieces of food, handling breakable items or navigating obstacles - it can surprise us to learn just how capable they are!
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: mbateman, exgenesis, now, good.

artifacts.title: Mbateman receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt:
it's also great for helping babies become joyful, confident, autonomous eaters who are aware of their own preferences and appetites
solid starts is a great blw resource: https://t.co/MtrO77f9ec
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: mbateman, exgenesis, now, good.

artifacts.title: Mbateman receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt: for newborns especially, the hands are a point of reference - some of the only things that are still constant from their "before life" in the womb. preserving their access to their hands (and filing nails frequently) helps them maintain this sense of familiarity and cosiness https://t.co/RHLRV1piIU
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: mbateman, exgenesis, now, good.

artifacts.title: Mbateman receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt: "While there is more risk with a young child holding a glass, there is also more opportunity to learn and develop respect, precision, and control of movement." read -> https://t.co/XJ5U1Sw3zk
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: mbateman, exgenesis, now, good.

planets.name: Montessori
planets.summary: Archive cluster around montessori: 263 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Montessori receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt:
some controversial baby-rearing practices that I think are pretty cool and based:

- montessori floor bed
- elimination communication
- baby-led weaning
- glass and ceramic tableware
- no socks or mittens
- minimal babyproofing

(my reasons why below 🧵) https://t.co/Vb6gdSsTBz
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: montessori, child, baby, guide.

artifacts.title: Montessori receipt from 2024-05-09
artifacts.excerpt: the biggest montessori pill is seeing under 2s pour themselves water from a glass pitcher to a glass cup, calmly get a towel to wipe up some that spilled, take the wet towel to a pail, come back to drink the water then carefully wash and dry their glass cup - all unprompted
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: montessori, child, baby, guide.

artifacts.title: Montessori receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt:
babies don't need cribs! a montessori floor bed is really just a thin mattress on the floor, and it has multiple benefits:
- freedom of movement
- autonomy
- spatial awareness
- easier for parent to lay down and cuddle baby
- safer (no bars or height)
- lower cost https://t.co/PlXUZuP7WA
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: montessori, child, baby, guide.

artifacts.title: Montessori receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt:
on the topic, in montessori we use REAL tableware with babies from the beginning: glass cups, ceramic plates, stainless steel utensils...
babies can use these just fine, and they help understand cause and effect - because misuse will result in loud sounds or even breaking https://t.co/WZPNgFd3iJ
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: montessori, child, baby, guide.

artifacts.title: Time receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt: ofc it's great to have some sections of the house be totally baby-safe ("Yes spaces"!), but babies also need to learn how to deal with obstacles. for example, they can learn to go up and down stairs safely if we give them time and space to practice: https://t.co/hcLkM2gK1w
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: time, think, going, every.

artifacts.title: Time receipt from 2024-06-16
artifacts.excerpt: babies are so cool. the stepping reflex is there from the start,  just gets a bit rusty from rapidly increasing bodyweight and lack of use. but newborns can move from the time they're born! https://t.co/iaRlwlb2CS
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: time, think, going, every.

planets.name: Baby
planets.summary: Archive cluster around baby: 211 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Baby receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt: (also, yes - baby will fall sleep on the floor sometimes... it's ok!) https://t.co/VkGUpN7wLk
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: baby, going, now, babies.

artifacts.title: Baby receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt:
baby-led weaning is basically just feeding your baby regular food (or a slightly modified) instead of special foods and purees! it helps:
- encourage the correct swallowing pattern
- eye hand coordination
- jaw development
- prevent picky eating
plus it's easier to prepare! https://t.co/ziwtPOUDaE
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: baby, going, now, babies.

artifacts.title: Baby receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt: elimination communication, or 'natural hygiene' is a practice based on learning and following your baby's cues wrt eliminating (peeing and pooping). it's often described as infant potty training, but in reality it's more like avoiding conditioning a baby to use a diaper https://t.co/JMnstzfpvB
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: baby, going, now, babies.

artifacts.title: Baby receipt from 2024-05-21
artifacts.excerpt:
this is also a really cool practice to try out if you can! some of the benefits are:
- increased (or... maintained?) bodily awareness for the baby
- increased awareness of the baby's communication for the parent
- less (especially poopy) diapers to throw out or wash
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: baby, going, now, babies.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2024-05-09

planets.name: Don

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2024-06-20
artifacts.excerpt: I don't understand how so many people, especially women, live without bidets. I mean real bidets like this not the tiny showers attached to a toilet. easily top 3 bathroom inventions https://t.co/6p8COA322X
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, want, feel.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2024-03-12
artifacts.excerpt: Since we’re on the topic, I love children and don’t like pretend play. It’s so boring 🤷‍♀️ I’ll play w/ a child for a bit if they ask me to, but then back off. I also distinctly remember as a child how pretend play was a magical space that could only be inhabited by other children.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, want, feel.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2024-08-28
artifacts.excerpt: I have this pet theory that babies' vestibular systems are "expecting" to be carried around by an adult on foot basically the whole day and when they don't get that input they need to be bounced and rocked
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, want, feel.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2024-06-03
artifacts.excerpt: I just want to put it out there that if you ask me anything at all about babies there’s a very high chance I'll have a good answer for you and if I don’t, I’ll be the most enthusiastic researcher ever 🫶🫶 I love learning and I love babies!
